Title: Evolutionary alterations in gene expression and enzymatic activities of gibberellin 3-oxidase 1 in Oryza

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports pollen viability, pollen tube germination, pollen tube elongation, and male fertility
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proper cell-wall retention and invertase activity of OsCIN3 promote sucrose-to-glucose conversion and pollen starch accumulation; defective retention/activity in osga3ox1 is associated with reduced starch and male sterility
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression correlation analysis; exogenous GA4 induction assay; fractionated invertase activity assays; western blot of fractionated anther proteins with OsCIN3 antibody; starch and sucrose metabolite measurements
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Appreciable expression correlation with OsGA3ox1 in anthers
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Anther/pollen-associated; induced by exogenous GA4 in wild type and osga3ox1
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public expression data/correlation analysis; accession NA
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve pollen starch loading and male fertility, but mechanism of GA-dependent cell-wall retention remains unresolved
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect carbohydrate partitioning and reproductive development; direct engineering implications not established
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not show downregulation of OsCIN3 transcript in osga3ox1; instead, it supports altered cell-wall retention/localization and reduced wall-associated invertase activity. Relationship is described as probable downstream target of OsGA3ox1-derived GAs.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCIN3 expression is correlated with OsGA3ox1 in anthers. 2. Exogenous GA4 strongly induces OsCIN3 expression. 3. osga3ox1 shows reduced wall-associated invertase activity and altered OsCIN3 partitioning among anther fractions. 4. These changes are associated with defective sucrose utilization and reduced pollen starch accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Non-13-hydroxylation pathway leading to bioactive GA4 and GA7
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 3β-hydroxylation of GA precursor GA9 to form bioactive GA4 and also produces GA7 at a relatively high ratio
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major anther-localized control point for GA4/GA7 accumulation in pollen; key control step in anther/pollen bioactive GA production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes pollen starch accumulation, pollen tube germination and elongation, anther filament elongation, anther dehiscence, and male fertility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of bioactive GA4 and especially GA7 in anthers; required for pollen starch accumulation and male fertility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; endogenous GA profiling; recombinant enzyme assay; promoter-GUS; complementation with GA3ox1 orthologs; exogenous GA rescue; pollen viability/germination assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed in late developmental stages of pollen, anther filaments, and lodicules; not in vegetative organs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA4 and GA7 rescue treatments; reproductive-stage anther development
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: OsCIN3 (probable downstream functional target); GID1; SLR1
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsGA3ox1 promoter for GUS and genomic complementation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public whole-genome data and expression datasets used for evolutionary/expression analyses; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for enhancing male fertility and pollen performance via anther-specific elevation of bioactive GA, especially GA7; anther-specific expression may avoid vegetative overgrowth penalties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ectopic or excessive GA production can cause harmful overgrowth; beneficial deployment likely requires reproductive tissue specificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A unique F235 residue in the 2OG-interacting site contributes to hyperproduction of GA7. The gene underwent evolutionary changes in both expression and enzyme activity within Oryza.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGA3ox1 is specifically expressed in late-stage anthers/pollen and is the major enzyme producing GA4 and GA7 in rice anthers. 2. osga3ox1 knockout reduces GA4 and GA7, increases GA9, and causes defective pollen starch accumulation, poor pollen germination/tube growth, and complete male sterility. 3. OsGA3ox1 produces GA7 at a higher ratio than OsGA3ox2, and GA7 more effectively rescues mutant pollen defects. 4. Evolution of OsGA3ox1 expression and catalytic properties in Oryza correlates with improved pollen starch accumulation and reproductive success.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: GA3ox1 orthologs (Oryza species and Leersia perrieri)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Various accessions including O. rufipogon W0106, O. punctata W1514, O. australiensis W0008, O. brachyantha W0654, O. granulata W0003, Leersia perrieri W1529
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: osga3ox1 mutant in Nipponbare background for complementation; native species for expression/activity comparison
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Orthologous GA3ox1 enzymes convert GA9 to GA4 and GA7 with species-dependent GA7/GA4 production ratios
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key evolutionary determinant of anther GA7 output and fertility performance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Associated with pollen starch accumulation and reproductive performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher GA7 productivity and stronger anther-specific expression in AA-genome Oryza orthologs are associated with better pollen starch accumulation and seed setting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; direct sequencing; recombinant enzyme assays; qRT-PCR across tissues/species; endogenous GA quantification; genomic complementation in osga3ox1 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AA-genome species show strong anther specificity/high expression; BB/EE/FF/GG and L. perrieri show broader and/or much lower expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In osga3ox1 complementation lines, expression level depends on ortholog used; L. perrieri ortholog low in anthers; O. granulata ortholog slightly higher in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Comparative analysis across Oryza evolution and reproductive tissues
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native genomic regions of each ortholog in complementation constructs
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Public whole-genome data; direct sequencing data of GA3ox1 from wild species; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: AA-genome/Oryza-type GA3ox1 alleles with strong anther-specific expression and higher GA7 productivity are promising alleles for improving pollen vigor and seed set
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Orthologs with broader expression may risk vegetative GA effects; low-expression alleles provide weak complementation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Y-to-F substitution in the 2OG-interacting site arose in the ancestor of FF-to-AA genomes and is associated with increased GA7 production. L. perrieri shows moderate GA7/GA4 despite Y-type residue, likely due to altered Arg315 orientation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GA3ox1 orthologs differ markedly in both expression pattern and GA7/GA4 production ratio across Oryza evolution. 2. AA-genome orthologs show highest anther-specific expression and GA7 accumulation. 3. Complementation strength in osga3ox1 correlates with ortholog GA7 productivity and anther expression. 4. Evolution of GA3ox1 likely contributed to reproductive advantage in Oryza.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of GA9 to bioactive GA4 and low amounts of GA7
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms bioactive GA but not the main target determinant in anthers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established for anther GA accumulation in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: General bioactive GA for plant growth and development
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces mainly GA4 and has lower GA7 synthesis ratio than OsGA3ox1; serves as comparator explaining OsGA3ox1 specialization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; X-ray crystallography of enzyme-substrate complex; expression analysis cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in various organs such as shoots and roots
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides structural benchmark for rational engineering of GA3ox catalytic specificity, especially GA7/GA4 ratio
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Null mutants previously reported to show severe dwarf phenotype, indicating strong vegetative growth effects
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal structure with GA9 and 2OG resolved at 1.9 Å; comparison with OsGA3ox1 identified F235/Y substitution as determinant of elevated GA7 production in OsGA3ox1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGA3ox2 mainly generates GA4 from GA9 with much less GA7 than OsGA3ox1. 2. Structural analysis of OsGA3ox2 provided the basis for identifying the catalytic site difference underlying OsGA3ox1 specialization. 3. OsGA3ox2 is broadly expressed and is not the major source of anther GA4/GA7 accumulation in this study.
